oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure audispd Plugin To Send Logs To Remote Server
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: remote_server setting in /etc/audit/audisp-remote.conf is set to a certain IP address or hostname

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure auditd Disk Full Action when Disk Space Is Full
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: disk_full_action setting in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to a certain action

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure auditd mail_acct Action on Low Disk Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: action_mail_acct setting in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to a certain account

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure auditd space_left Action on Low Disk Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: space_left_action setting in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to a certain action

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure auditd space_left on Low Disk Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: space_left setting in /etc/audit/auditd.conf is set to at least a certain value

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Offload audit Logs to External Media
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Check if a script for audit offload exists in /etc/cron.weekly/

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Disable unauthenticated repositories in APT configuration
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Accessing a repository should be allowed only when the repository is authenticated.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check if allow-unauthenticated is set to false or is undefined

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check if allow-unauthenticated is set to false or is undefined

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Configure the maxpoll setting in /etc/ntp.conf or chrony.conf to continuously poll the time source servers.

oval-def:criterion.comment: check if maxpoll is set in /etc/ntp.conf
check if all server entries have maxpoll set in /etc/ntp.conf
check if maxpoll is set in /etc/chrony/chrony.conf or /etc/chrony/conf.d/
check if all server entries have maxpoll set in /etc/chrony/chrony.conf or /etc/chrony/conf.d/

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Certificate trust path in SSSD
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: SSSD should be configured with trust path to an accepted trust anchor.

oval-def:criterion.comment: check value of certificate_verification in sssd configuration

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure PAM in SSSD Services
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: SSSD should be configured to run SSSD PAM services.

oval-def:criterion.comment: check if pam is configured in the services setting of the sssd section

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Smartcards in SSSD
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: SSSD should be configured to authenticate access to the system using smart cards.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Certificates Mapping in SSSD
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: SSSD should be configured to map the certificate to correct user or group

oval-def:criterion.comment: check value of ldap_user_certificate in sssd configuration

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure SSSD to Expire Offline Credentials
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: SSSD should be configured to expire offline credentials after 1 day.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable authselect
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Check that authselect is enabled

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check that authselect symlinks are set up properly.

oval-def:criterion.comment: The 'fingerprint-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'password-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'postlogin' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'smartcard-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'system-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Modify the System Login Banner for Remote Connections
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The system login banner text should be set correctly.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Account Lockouts Must Be Logged
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Account Lockouts Must Be Logged

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected value for pam_faillock.so audit parameter
Check expected pam_faillock.so audit parameter in pam files

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the audit parameter in auth section of system-auth file
Check the audit parameter in auth section of password-auth file
Ensure /etc/security/faillock.conf is not used together with pam files

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected pam_faillock.so audit parameter in faillock.conf

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the audit parameter is not present system-auth file
Check the audit parameter is not present password-auth file
Ensure the audit parameter is present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Do Not Show System Messages When Unsuccessful Logon Attempts Occur
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Prevent System Messages When Three Unsuccessful Logon Attempts Occur

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected value for pam_faillock.so silent parameter
Check expected pam_faillock.so silent parameter in pam files

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the silent parameter in auth section of system-auth file
Check the silent parameter in auth section of password-auth file
Ensure /etc/security/faillock.conf is not used together with pam files

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected pam_faillock.so silent parameter in faillock.conf

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the silent parameter is not present system-auth file
Check the silent parameter is not present password-auth file
Ensure the silent parameter is present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Authentication Retry Prompts Permitted Per-Session
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The password retry should meet minimum requirements

oval-def:criteria.comment: The password retry should meet minimum requirements
Conditions for retry are satisfied
Conditions for retry in PAM files are satisfied

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_pwquality has correctly set the retry argument in system-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Conditions for retry in /etc/security/pwquality.conf file are satisfied

oval-def:criterion.comment: retry value not set in PAM files

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Password Hashing Algorithm for PAM
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The password hashing algorithm should be set correctly in {{{ pam_file }}}.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Password Hashing Algorithm in /etc/login.defs
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The password hashing algorithm should be set correctly in /usr/etc/login.defs.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del Reboot Activation
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: By default, the system will reboot when the Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence is pressed.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Account Expiration Following Inactivity
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The accounts should be configured to expire automatically following password expiration.

oval-def:criteria.comment: the value INACTIVE parameter should be set appropriately in /etc/default/useradd

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Password Maximum Age
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The maximum password age policy should meet minimum requirements.

oval-def:criteria.comment: The value PASS_MAX_DAYS should be set appropriately in /etc/login.defs

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Password Minimum Age
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The minimum password age policy should be set appropriately.

oval-def:criteria.comment: The value of PASS_MIN_DAYS should be set appropriately in /etc/login.defs

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set number of Password Hashing Rounds - password-auth
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The number of rounds for password hashing should be set correctly.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check if rounds option of pam_unix is as expected

oval-def:criterion.comment: The value of rounds is set correctly in pam_unix.so

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Prevent Login to Accounts With Empty Password
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The file /etc/pam.d/system-auth should not contain the nullok option

oval-def:criterion.comment: make sure the nullok option is not used in /etc/pam.d/system-auth

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure There Are No Accounts With Blank or Null Passwords
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The file /etc/shadow shows that there aren't empty passwords

oval-def:criterion.comment: make sure there aren't blank or null passwords in /etc/shadow

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Direct root Logins Are Not Allowed
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Direct root Logins Are Not Allowed

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Limit the Number of Concurrent Login Sessions Allowed Per User
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The maximum number of concurrent login sessions per user should meet minimum requirements.

oval-def:criterion.comment: the value maxlogins should be set appropriately in /etc/security/limits.d/*.conf
the value maxlogins should not be set at all in /etc/security/limits.d/*.conf
the value maxlogins should be set appropriately in /etc/security/limits.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Interactive Session Timeout
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks interactive shell timeout

oval-def:criterion.comment: At least one config file has TMOUT defined
All configured TMOUT values must be >= 1

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Boot Loader Password in grub2
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The grub2 boot loader should have password protection enabled.

oval-def:criteria.comment: check both files to account for procedure change in documentation

oval-def:criterion.comment: make sure a password is defined in /boot/grub/user.cfg
make sure a password is defined in /boot/grub/grub.cfg
make sure a superuser is defined in /boot/grub/grub.cfg

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set the UEFI Boot Loader Password
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The UEFI grub2 boot loader should have password protection enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: make sure a password is defined in /boot/efi/EFI/ubuntu/user.cfg

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure real-time clock is set to UTC
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Ensure RTC is using UTC as its time base

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check softlink exists for /etc/localtime and look for UTC pattern

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure remote access methods are monitored in Rsyslog
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Rsyslog should be configured to monitor remote access methods.

oval-def:criteria.comment: remote access methods are configured in rsyslog

oval-def:criterion.comment: ensure 'auth.*' remote method is configured in rsyslog
ensure 'authpriv.*' remote method is configured in rsyslog
ensure 'daemon.*' remote method is configured in rsyslog

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Deactivate Wireless Network Interfaces
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: All wireless interfaces should be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: check if wifi interfaces are disabled

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that All World-Writable Directories Have Sticky Bits Set
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The sticky bit should be set for all world-writable directories.

oval-def:criterion.comment: All local world-writable directories have sticky bit set

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that system commands files are group owned by root or a system account
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks that system commands in /bin /sbin /usr/bin /usr/sbin /usr/local/bin /usr/local/sbin are owned by root group or a system account.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that System Executables Have Root Ownership
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks that /bin, /sbin, /usr/bin, /usr/sbin, /usr/local/bin, /usr/local/sbin, /usr/libexec, and objects therein, are owned by root.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that System Executables Have Restrictive Permissions
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks that binary files under /bin, /sbin, /usr/bin, /usr/sbin, /usr/local/bin, /usr/local/sbin, and /usr/libexec are not group-writable or world-writable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable NX or XD Support in the BIOS
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The NX (no-execution) bit flag should be set on the system.

oval-def:criterion.comment: NX is not disabled in the kernel command line

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify '/proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled' exists
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Inspect the contents of /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure Users Re-Authenticate for Privilege Escalation - sudo !authenticate
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks sudo usage without authentication

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure Users Re-Authenticate for Privilege Escalation - sudo NOPASSWD
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Checks sudo usage without password

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: The operating system must restrict privilege elevation to authorized personnel
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Check that sudoers doesn't allow all users to run commands via sudo

oval-def:criterion.comment: Make sure that sudoers has restrictions on which users can run sudo for any target user
Make sure that sudoers has restrictions on which users can run sudo for any target group

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure apt_get Removes Previous Package Versions
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The clean_requirements_on_remove option should be used to ensure that old versions of software components are removed after updating.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Enforcing
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Check presence of enforcing = 1 in /etc/security/pwquality.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: Test conditions - presence of the file plus 0 extra definitions.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check that /etc/security/pwquality.conf contains a line with certain text

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enforce Delay After Failed Logon Attempts
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Configure PAM module

oval-def:criteria.comment: Make sure arguments are properly configured in /etc/pam.d/common-auth

oval-def:criterion.comment: Verify delay is set to the desired state

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Lock Accounts After Failed Password Attempts
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Lockout account after failed login attempts.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check the proper configuration of pam_faillock.so
Check if pam_faillock.so is properly enabled
Count occurrences of pam_unix.so in system-auth and password-auth

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of system-auth
pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check common definition of pam_faillock.so

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of password-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected value for pam_faillock.so deny parameter
Check expected pam_faillock.so deny parameter in pam files

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the deny parameter in auth section of system-auth file
Check the deny parameter in auth section of password-auth file
Ensure the deny parameter is not present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected pam_faillock.so deny parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the deny parameter is not present system-auth file
Check the deny parameter is not present password-auth file
Ensure the deny parameter is present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Interval For Counting Failed Password Attempts
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The number of allowed failed logins should be set correctly.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check the proper configuration of pam_faillock.so
Check if pam_faillock.so is properly enabled
Count occurrences of pam_unix.so in system-auth and password-auth

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of system-auth
pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check common definition of pam_faillock.so

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of password-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected value for pam_faillock.so fail_interval parameter
Check expected pam_faillock.so fail_interval parameter in pam files

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the fail_interval parameter in auth section of system-auth file
Check the fail_interval parameter in auth section of password-auth file
Ensure the fail_interval parameter is not present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected pam_faillock.so fail_interval parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the fail_interval parameter is not present system-auth file
Check the fail_interval parameter is not present password-auth file
Ensure the fail_interval parameter is present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Set Lockout Time for Failed Password Attempts
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The unlock time after number of failed logins should be set correctly.

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check the proper configuration of pam_faillock.so
Check if pam_faillock.so is properly enabled
Count occurrences of pam_unix.so in system-auth and password-auth

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of system-auth
pam_unix.so appears only once in auth section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check common definition of pam_faillock.so

oval-def:criterion.comment: pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of system-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in auth section of password-auth
pam_faillock.so is properly defined in account section of password-auth

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected value for pam_faillock.so unlock_time parameter
Check expected pam_faillock.so unlock_time parameter in pam files

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the unlock_time parameter in auth section of system-auth file
Check the unlock_time parameter in auth section of password-auth file
Ensure the unlock_time parameter is not present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: Check expected pam_faillock.so unlock_time parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check the unlock_time parameter is not present system-auth file
Check the unlock_time parameter is not present password-auth file
Ensure the unlock_time parameter is present in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Configure AIDE To Notify Personnel if Baseline Configurations Are Altered
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Ensure 'SILENTREPORTS' is configured with value 'no' in /etc/default/aide

oval-def:criteria.comment: The respective application or service is configured correctly and configuration file exists
The respective application or service is configured correctly

oval-def:criterion.comment: test if configuration file /etc/default/aide exists for aide_disable_silentreports

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Ensure AppArmor is Active and Configured
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The apparmor service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package apparmor-parser installed and service apparmor is configured to start
service apparmor is configured to start and is running
service apparmor is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Synchronize internal information system clocks
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Ensure 'makestep' is configured with value '1 -1' in /etc/chrony/chrony.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: The respective application or service is configured correctly and configuration file exists
The respective application or service is configured correctly

oval-def:criterion.comment: test if configuration file /etc/chrony/chrony.conf exists for chronyd_sync_clock

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that Shared Library Directories Have Root Group Ownership
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /lib/, /lib64/, /usr/lib/, /usr/lib64/ is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify group-owner of system journal directories
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /run/log/journal/, /var/log/journal/ is group owned by systemd-journal.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that system commands directories are group owned by root
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /bin/, /sbin/, /usr/bin/, /usr/sbin/, /usr/local/bin/, /usr/local/sbin/ is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify owner of system journal directories
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /run/log/journal/, /var/log/journal/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that System Executable Have Root Ownership
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /bin/, /sbin/, /usr/bin/, /usr/sbin/, /usr/local/bin/, /usr/local/sbin/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that Shared Library Directories Have Root Ownership
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /lib/, /lib64/, /usr/lib/, /usr/lib64/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that System Executable Directories Have Restrictive Permissions
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /bin/, /sbin/, /usr/bin/, /usr/sbin/, /usr/local/bin/, /usr/local/sbin/ has mode 0755. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on the system journal directories
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /run/log/journal/, /var/log/journal/ has mode 2750. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Groupowner on the journalctl command
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /usr/bin/journalctl is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Group Who Owns the system journal
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that ^/var/log/journal/.*/system.journal$ is group owned by systemd-journal.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log Directory
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/ is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log/syslog File
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/syslog is group owned by 4.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Audit Configuration Files Must Be Owned By Group root
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /etc/audit/, /etc/audit/rules.d/ is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Owner on the journalctl Command
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /usr/bin/journalctl is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Owner on the system journal
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that ^/var/log/journal/.*/system.journal$ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log Directory
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log/syslog File
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/syslog is owned by syslog.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that audit tools are owned by root
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /sbin/auditctl, /sbin/aureport, /sbin/ausearch, /sbin/autrace, /sbin/auditd, /sbin/audispd, /sbin/augenrules is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Audit Configuration Files Must Be Owned By Root
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /etc/audit/, /etc/audit/rules.d/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that Shared Library Files Have Root Ownership
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /lib/, /lib64/, /usr/lib/, /usr/lib64/ is owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that audit tools Have Mode 0755 or less
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /sbin/auditctl, /sbin/aureport, /sbin/ausearch, /sbin/autrace, /sbin/auditd, /sbin/audispd, /sbin/augenrules has mode 0755. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on /etc/audit/auditd.conf
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /etc/audit/auditd.conf has mode 0640. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on /etc/audit/audit.rules
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /etc/audit/audit.rules has mode 0640. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on /etc/audit/rules.d/*.rules
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /etc/audit/rules.d/ has mode 0600. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on the journal command
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /usr/bin/journalctl has mode 0740. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that Shared Library Files Have Restrictive Permissions
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /lib/, /lib64/, /usr/lib/, /usr/lib64/ has mode 7755. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on the system journal
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that ^/var/log/journal/.*/system.journal$ has mode 0640. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on /var/log Directory
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/ has mode 0755. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/syslog File
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/syslog has mode 0640. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Disable Modprobe Loading of USB Storage Driver
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel module usb-storage should be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel module usb-storage disabled in modprobe.d

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify permissions of log files
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /var/log/ has mode 0640. If the target file or directory has an extended ACL, then it will fail the mode check.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify the system-wide library files in directories "/lib", "/lib64", "/usr/lib/" and "/usr/lib64" are group-owned by root.
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: This test makes sure that /lib/, /lib64/, /usr/lib/, /usr/lib64/ is group owned by 0.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable auditd Service
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The auditd service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package audit installed and service auditd is configured to start
service auditd is configured to start and is running
service auditd is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable rsyslog Service
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The rsyslog service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package rsyslog installed and service rsyslog is configured to start
service rsyslog is configured to start and is running
service rsyslog is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable the OpenSSH Service
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The sshd service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package openssh-server installed and service sshd is configured to start
service sshd is configured to start and is running
service sshd is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable the SSSD Service
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The sssd service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package sssd-common installed and service sssd is configured to start
service sssd is configured to start and is running
service sssd is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify ufw Enabled
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The ufw service should be enabled if possible.

oval-def:criteria.comment: package ufw installed and service ufw is configured to start
service ufw is configured to start and is running
service ufw is configured to start

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Restrict Access to Kernel Message Buffer
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The 'kernel.dmesg_restrict' kernel parameter should be set to the appropriate value in system configuration and system runtime.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Restrict Access to Kernel Message Buffer
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'kernel.dmesg_restrict' parameter should be set to 1 in the system runtime.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel runtime parameter kernel.dmesg_restrict set to 1

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Restrict Access to Kernel Message Buffer
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'kernel.dmesg_restrict' parameter should be set to 1 in the system configuration.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel static parameter kernel.dmesg_restrict set to 1 in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter kernel.dmesg_restrict missing in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter kernel.dmesg_restrict set to 1 in sysctl files managed by packages

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The 'kernel.randomize_va_space' kernel parameter should be set to the appropriate value in system configuration and system runtime.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'kernel.randomize_va_space' parameter should be set to 2 in the system runtime.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel runtime parameter kernel.randomize_va_space set to 2

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Randomized Layout of Virtual Address Space
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'kernel.randomize_va_space' parameter should be set to 2 in the system configuration.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel static parameter kernel.randomize_va_space set to 2 in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter kernel.randomize_va_space missing in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter kernel.randomize_va_space set to 2 in sysctl files managed by packages

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Use TCP Syncookies on Network Interfaces
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The 'net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies' kernel parameter should be set to the appropriate value in system configuration and system runtime.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Use TCP Syncookies on Network Interfaces
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies' parameter should be set to the appropriate value in the system runtime.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel runtime parameter net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies set to the appropriate value

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Enable Kernel Parameter to Use TCP Syncookies on Network Interfaces
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The kernel 'net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies' parameter should be set to the appropriate value in the system configuration.

oval-def:criterion.comment: kernel static parameter net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies set to the appropriate value in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies missing in sysctl files not managed by packages
kernel static parameter net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies set to the appropriate value in sysctl files managed by packages

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: Verify that 'use_mappers' is set to 'pwent' in PAM
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Check presence of use_mappers = pwent in /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf

oval-def:criteria.comment: Test conditions - presence of the file plus 0 extra definitions.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Check that /etc/pam_pkcs11/pam_pkcs11.conf contains a line with certain text

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: remote server to send audit records
disk error action
email account for actions
space left action
admin space left action
/etc/cron.weekly/audit-offload exists
Checks allow-unauthenticated in apt configs
check if maxpoll is set in /etc/ntp.conf
check if maxpoll is set in /etc/chrony/chrony.conf or /etc/chrony/conf.d/
check if all server entries have maxpoll set in /etc/ntp.conf
check if all server entries have maxpoll set in /etc/chrony/chrony.conf or /etc/chrony/conf.d/
test the value of certificate_verification in sssd configuration
check if pam is configured in the services setting of the sssd section
tests the value of pam_cert_auth setting in the /etc/sssd/sssd.conf file
test the value of ldap_user_certificate in sssd configuration
tests the value of offline_credentials_expiration setting in the /etc/sssd/sssd.conf file

unix:symlink_test.comment: The 'fingerprint-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'password-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'postlogin' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'smartcard-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart
The 'system-auth' PAM config is a symlink to its authselect counterpart

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: correct banner in /etc/issue.net
Check the presence of audit parameter in system-auth
Check the absence of audit parameter in system-auth
Check the presence of audit parameter in password-auth
Check the absence of audit parameter in password-auth
Check the expected audit value in in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the absence of audit parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the presence of silent parameter in system-auth
Check the absence of silent parameter in system-auth
Check the presence of silent parameter in password-auth
Check the absence of silent parameter in password-auth
Check the expected silent value in in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the absence of silent parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf
check the configuration of /etc/pam.d/system-auth
check the configuration of /etc/pam.d/system-auth
check the configuration of /etc/security/pwquality.conf
check if pam_unix.so hashing algorithm option is correct and specified only once in /etc/pam.d/common-password

ind:variable_test.comment: The value of ENCRYPT_METHOD should be set appropriately in /etc/login.defs

unix:symlink_test.comment: Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence override exists

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: the value INACTIVE parameter should be set appropriately in /etc/default/useradd

ind:variable_test.comment: The value of PASS_MAX_DAYS should be set appropriately in /etc/login.defs
The value of PASS_MIN_DAYS should be set appropriately in /etc/login.defs

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: Test if rounds attribute of pam_unix.so is set correctly in /etc/pam.d/password-auth
make sure nullok is not used in /etc/pam.d/system-auth
make sure there aren't blank or null passwords in /etc/shadow
make sure root account is locked in /etc/shadow
the value maxlogins should be set appropriately in /etc/security/limits.conf
the value maxlogins should be set appropriately in /etc/security/limits.d/*.conf
the value maxlogins should be set appropriately in /etc/security/limits.d/*.conf
TMOUT in /etc/profile
TMOUT in /etc/profile.d/*.sh

ind:variable_test.comment: Check that at least one TMOUT is defined

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: All TMOUT values must be greater than or equal to 1
superuser is defined in /boot/grub/grub.cfg files.
make sure a password is defined in /boot/grub/user.cfg
make sure a password is defined in /boot/grub/grub.cfg
make sure a password is defined in /boot/efi/EFI/ubuntu/user.cfg

unix:symlink_test.comment: Ensure softlink exist for localtime with UTC pattern

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: remote method auth monitoring configured in rsyslog'
remote method authpriv monitoring configured in rsyslog'
remote method daemon monitoring configured in rsyslog'

unix:interface_test.comment: check if UP flag is present on wifi interfaces

unix:file_test.comment: Check the existence of world-writable directories without sticky bits
system commands are owned by root or a system account

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: CPUs support for NX bit
NX is not disabled in the kernel command line
Tests if contents of /proc/sys/crypto/fips_enabled is exactly what is defined in rule description
!authenticate does not exist in /etc/sudoers
!authenticate does not exist in /etc/sudoers.d
NOPASSWD does not exist /etc/sudoers
NOPASSWD does not exist in /etc/sudoers.d
Make sure that sudoers has restrictions on which users can run sudo
Make sure that sudoers has restrictions on which users can run sudo
check value of clean_requirements_on_remove in /etc/apt/apt.conf
tests the presence of 'enforcing = 1' setting in the /etc/security/pwquality.conf file
Verify delay configuation of pam_faildelay.so
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of system-auth
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in password-auth
Check the absence of deny parameter in system-auth
Check the expected deny value in system-auth
Check the absence of deny parameter in password-auth
Check the expected deny value in password-auth
Check the expected deny value in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the absence of deny parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of system-auth
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in password-auth
Check the absence of fail_interval parameter in system-auth
Check the expected fail_interval value in system-auth
Check the absence of fail_interval parameter in password-auth
Check the expected fail_interval value in password-auth
Check the expected fail_interval value in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the absence of fail_interval parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of system-auth
no more that one pam_unix.so is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in auth section of password-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in system-auth
One and only one occurrence is expected in password-auth
Check the absence of unlock_time parameter in system-auth
Check the expected unlock_time value in system-auth
Check the absence of unlock_time parameter in password-auth
Check the expected unlock_time value in password-auth
Check the expected unlock_time value in /etc/security/faillock.conf
Check the absence of unlock_time parameter in /etc/security/faillock.conf
tests the value of SILENTREPORTS setting in the /etc/default/aide file

unix:file_test.comment: The configuration file /etc/default/aide exists for aide_disable_silentreports

linux:systemdunitproperty_test.comment: Test that the apparmor service is running

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: tests the value of makestep setting in the /etc/chrony/chrony.conf file

unix:file_test.comment: The configuration file /etc/chrony/chrony.conf exists for chronyd_sync_clock

ind:textfilecontent54_test.comment: kernel module usb-storage disabled
kernel module usb-storage disabled in /etc/modprobe.conf

linux:systemdunitproperty_test.comment: Test that the auditd service is running
Test that the rsyslog service is running
Test that the sshd service is running
Test that the sssd service is running
Test that the ufw service is running

Get the second and subsequent occurrences of pam_unix.so in auth section of system-auth

oval-def:local_variable.comment: Build regex to be case insensitive
Build regex to be case insensitive

oval-def:constant_variable.comment: regex to identify audit parameter in pam files
regex to identify silent parameter in pam files

oval-def:local_variable.comment: The value of last ENCRYPT_METHOD directive in /etc/login.defs
The value of last PASS_MAX_DAYS directive in /etc/login.defs
The value of last PASS_MIN_DAYS directive in /etc/login.defs

oval-def:external_variable.comment: maximum number of concurrent logins per user

oval-def:constant_variable.comment: regex to identify pam_unix.so in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entries in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entry in account section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so deny entry in auth section of pam files
regex to identify deny entry in /etc/security/faillock.conf
regex to identify pam_unix.so in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entries in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entry in account section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so fail_interval entry in auth section of pam files
regex to identify fail_interval entry in /etc/security/faillock.conf
regex to identify pam_unix.so in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entries in auth section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so entry in account section of pam files
regex to identify pam_faillock.so unlock_time entry in auth section of pam files
regex to identify unlock_time entry in /etc/security/faillock.conf

oval-def:local_variable.comment: Retrieve the gid of systemd-journal from either /etc/group or /usr/lib/group
Retrieve the gid of systemd-journal from either /etc/group or /usr/lib/group

oval-def:constant_variable.comment: Other paths where kernel modules can be configured
